description: California's Proposition 37 aimed to mandate the labeling of genetically modified foods. Despite high initial support, a massive multi-million dollar advertising campaign by chemical and food corporations turned public opinion, leading to its defeat.

key_events.description: Opposition campaign launches 'No on 37' commercials citing increased food prices.
Debate intensifies over 'right to know' versus 'scientific consensus' on safety.
Major food companies spend record-breaking sums on media buys in final weeks.

calibration_notes: Excellent for studying the impact of asymmetric funding on voter sentiment in direct democracy.
